CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

San Francisco Market Corporationss Notice Of Motion And Motion In Opposition To Plaintiffs Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-18-570237 Georgia Vardakastanis VS. San Francisco Produce Association and
CGC-19-579287 Georgia Vardakastanis, et al., VS. Eleasia Fraise, et al.

Appearances:
Doris Cheng, esq and Jeffrey Clause, esq. (no number provided) of Walkup Melodia... for Plaintiff via court call.
David Delbon, esq. (415-554-3962) of San Francisco Deputy City Attorney for Defendant City of San Francisco via court call.
Christopher Garrett, esq. (925-459-8440) of Guichard...& Garrett A.P.C. for Defendant SF Market Corporation via court call.
Gregory Doyle, esq. (510-835-3400 x.228) of Toschi...& Doyle for Defendants, Monique Smith, et al, via court call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MARKET CORPORATION's Motion In Opposition To Plaintiffs Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement:

Plaintiffs' application for determination of good faith settlement is granted. Plaintiffs show that defendants Monique Smith and Lynnique Smith have tendered $15,000, the limits of their available liability insurance coverage, and their declarations show that they have very modest assets. (Lynnique Smith Decl. 1, 4; Monique C. Morris-Smith Decl. 1, 6; see Dole Food Co., Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 894, 909 [relevant considerations include the financial conditions and insurance policy limits of settling defendants]; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1149, 1152 ["Notwithstanding the settlement's disproportionality, because Guerrero lacks other assets, substantial evidence supports the finding of good faith settlement"].) Under the circumstances, Defendant fails to meet its burden to show that the settlement is not in good faith. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d) [the party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue]; Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion Of Opposition To Plaintiffs Application For Detemination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-18-570237 Georgia Vardakastanis VS. San Francisco Produce Association and
CGC-19-579287 Georgia Vardakastanis, et al., VS. Eleasia Fraise, et al.

Appearances:
Doris Cheng, esq and Jeffrey Clause, esq. (no number provided) of Walkup Melodia... for Plaintiff via court call.
David Delbon, esq. (415-554-3962) of San Francisco Deputy City Attorney for Defendant City of San Francisco via court call.
Christopher Garrett, esq. (925-459-8440) of Guichard...& Garrett A.P.C. for Defendant SF Market Corporation via court call.
Gregory Doyle, esq. (510-835-3400 x.228) of Toschi...& Doyle for Defendants, Monique Smith, et al, via court call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

REAL PARTY IN INTEREST CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion Of Opposition To Plaintiffs Application For Detemination Of Good Faith Settlement:

Plaintiffs' application for determination of good faith settlement is granted. Plaintiffs show that defendants Monique Smith and Lynnique Smith have tendered $15,000, the limits of their available liability insurance coverage, and their declarations show that they have very modest assets. (Lynnique Smith Decl. 1, 4; Monique C. Morris-Smith Decl. 1, 6; see Dole Food Co., Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 894, 909 [relevant considerations include the financial conditions and insurance policy limits of settling defendants]; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1149, 1152 ["Notwithstanding the settlement's disproportionality, because Guerrero lacks other assets, substantial evidence supports the finding of good faith settlement"].) Under the circumstances, Defendant fails to meet its burden to show that the settlement is not in good faith. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d) [the party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue]; Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
